mission.title: signoz: [web] access frontend from a different basepath than root
mission.description: [web] access frontend from a different basepath than root. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current signoz deployment
steps.description:
Verify your signoz version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz
helm list -n signoz 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working signoz installation.

steps.title: Review signoz configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant signoz configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz
kubectl get configmap -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=signoz
```
## Is your feature request related to a problem?

I'm behind an argo tunnel and traefik, and I need the frontend to be accessed from "http://siteurl/admin/signoz". Right now, this results in 404 errors.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [web] access frontend from a different basepath than root
steps.description:
Hi folks,
Base path support is live in SigNoz **v0.121.0**. You can now serve SigNoz under any URL prefix.

Check out the [Serving SigNoz on an external URL](https://signoz.io/docs/manage/administrator-guide/configuration/serving-on-external-url/) doc.

PS: We've also been building
```yaml
signoz-frontend:
    image: signoz/frontend:0.4.3
    container_name: frontend
    depends_on:
      - query-service
    links:
      - "query-service"
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - ./signoz/nginx-config.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    environment:
      - BASE_PATH=/admin/signoz
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.signoz.rule=PathPrefix(`/admin/signoz`)
      - traefik.http.services.signoz.loadbalancer.server.port=3000

  traefik:
    image: traefik:latest
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 8181:8080 #traefik dashboard
    volumes:

```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n signoz -l app.kubernetes.io/name=signoz
kubectl get events -n signoz --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[web] access frontend from a different basepath than root" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hi folks,
Base path support is live in SigNoz **v0.121.0**. You can now serve SigNoz under any URL prefix.

Check out the [Serving SigNoz on an external URL](https://signoz.io/docs/manage/administrator-guide/configuration/serving-on-external-url/) doc.

PS: We've also been building [Foundry](https://github.com/SigNoz/foundry), a unified install tool for SigNoz.
codeSnippets:
signoz-frontend:
    image: signoz/frontend:0.4.3
    container_name: frontend
    depends_on:
      - query-service
    links:
      - "query-service"
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - ./signoz/nginx-config.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    environment:
      - BASE_PATH=/admin/signoz
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.signoz.rule=PathPrefix(`/admin/signoz`)
      - traefik.http.services.signoz.loadbalancer.server.port=3000

  traefik:
    image: traefik:latest
    ports:
      - 80:80
      - 8181:8080 #traefik dashboard
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    command:
      - "--log.level=ERROR"
      - "--api=true" #traefik dashboard
      - "--providers.docker=true"
      - "--providers.docker.expo

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with signoz installed or the issue environment reproducible.
